def: v. n. 1. To dispute ( bahsnā , 2). 1. Are mīyāṅ, le bhī lo, kyoṅ jhagaṛte ho? 2. Paise paise pe yūṅ jhagaṛte hain, Jaise āpas meṅ gair laṛte haiṅ . For every pice they quarrel so As strangers 'mongst themselves will do. 2. To argue ( bahsnā , 1). 3. To insist upon; raise one's demands. 1. Āj hī to mere jhagaṛne kā din hai . 2. Ab na jhagreṅ to aur kab jhagṛeṅ? I must fight for my present now or never.
adj. & s.m. (dialec.) = jhagṛālū, q.v. adj. & s.m. Quarrelsome, wrangling, disputatious, contentious, litigious; — a disputer, an arguer; a contentious or quarrelsome person, a brawler.

subs: To raise a quarrel, to set a-wrangling, &c. = jhagṛā karānā

wety: From Prakrit *𑀛𑀕𑁆𑀕𑀟 (*jhaggaḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), a reconstructed variant of 𑀛𑀕𑀟 (jhagaḍa). See झगड़ना (jhagaṛnā) for more. Cognates Cognate with Punjabi ਝਗੜਾ / جھَگْڑَا (jhagṛā), Sindhi جَهڳڙِو (jhaɠṛo), جهيڙو (jheṛo), Bengali ঝগড়া (jhogoṛa), Assamese জগৰ (zogor).

def: n. m. See بکهيرَا 1—8, 12. Wrangling; strife; quarrel; brawl. 1. Lage ragṛā, miṭe jhagṛā . Bhangaṛs. If you begin to pound strife will cease to sound. (Let the toper have his glass, his ill-temper will pass.) 2. Bāp beṭā ke jhagṛā na phāve . Bhoj. Prov. For father and son to fight's not pleasing to the sight. 3. Rūs, Rūm kā jhagṛā paise ko! Delhi street cry. 4. Sab jīte jī ke jhagṛe haiṅ, sach pūchho to kyā khāk huē, Jab maut se-ā-kar kām paṛā sab qisse qazye pāk huë . Nazīr. All life is one long strife, the end whereof is only dust, When death the arbiter appears the strife is ended just. jhagṛā pāk honā, yā chuknā , 1. To be settled (a quarrel). 2. To die. jhagṛā karānā, yā uṭhānā , v. a. To make a quarrel; sow dissension; foment discord. binā jhagṛe , adj. Undisputed.
s.f. A quarrelsome or contentious woman.
more: f. adj. Quarrelsome, wrangling, disputatious, contentious; a brawler, an arguer.

n. m. 1. Troubled or disturbed (water). 2. Quarrel; strife; contention. He nāyan, taiṅ kyā kahā kar jhūṭā jhak-jhol; Merā nimānā lāḍlā kyā jāne mukh bol? Chaubolā. O barber's wife! how can you such big lies invent, My boy's a simple quiet child—dear innocent!

n. f. jhak-jhol , n. m. Pulling and hauling; sky-larking; dalliance. Ek samaë Brij kī sab banitā harakh chalīṅ jal orī, Manjan hetu dhasī Jamnā meṅ, koī sāṅvari, koī gorī, Kareṅ jal men jhak-jhorī, Sāṅvare ko charitr suno rī . Brij. Wom. song. The maids of Brij once one and all with transport gained the stream, In Jamna's waters all immersed, some brown, some fair did seem, In wanton play they splashed and ducked, Kanhaiya's sports my theme!
more: s. f. Scrambling and wrangling, snatching.

def: s.f. = H جهکولا झकोला jhakolā, s.m. A shaking, agitation; a shock, &c. = jhakor and jhakorā; splashing, a splash or dip (in water); a large wave ( = jhakorā)
more: (from جهکولنا) s. f. The act of shaking in the water.

r: jhakolā, jhakol, jhakor, jhakorā, jhakolā denā, ko, jhakolnā
def: s.m. = H جهکول झकोल jhakol, s.f. A shaking, agitation; a shock, &c. = jhakor and jhakorā; splashing, a splash or dip (in water); a large wave ( = jhakorā)
more: n. m. 1. A wave. 2. A splash or dip in the water. 1. Ek aisā jhakolā āyā ki sab kuchh bahā le gayā . 2. Gaṅgā ke nau jhakole. Rām! Jamnā ure, Gaṅgā pare, aur bīch bahe daryāo. Rādhā piyārī he! benā jhakole ṭhanḍe nīr ke. Pahlā jhakolā mere Rām kā jin yeh sarishṭ upāī. Rādhā piyārī he! Dūjā jhakolā mere bāp kā jin maṅḍhā chhavāyā. Rādhā piyārī he! Tījā jhakolā merī mā kā jo bojh-marī dus mās. Rādhā piyārī he! Chauthā jhakolā mere bīr kā ham upje ek odar. Rādhā piyārī he! Pāṅchvāṅ jhakolā merī bahan kā jin god khilāī. Rādhā piyārī he! Chhaṭā jhakolā mere sasur kā jin biyāhe do dal joṛ. Rādhā piyārī he! Sātvāṅ jhakolā merī sās kā jiṅ sauṅp dīyā ghar bār. Rādhā piyārī he! Āṭhvāṅ jhakolā mere jeṭh kā jin bāṅṭ līyā ghar bār. Rādhā piyārī he! Navāṅ jhakolā mere purakh kā jo lāyā thā sir dhar mor. Rādhā piyārī he! Rus. Hin. Wom. Hymn. The female pilgrim's nine dips in the Ganges. The Jamna hithe…

def: v.t. To shake, put in motion, &c. ( = jhakornā); to dash or knock about (in water); to splash.

v. n. 1. To glitter, to glare. 2. To be in a passion. 3. To throb, to ache, to smart.

(causal of جهلکنا) v. a. To cause to shine, brighten, burnish.

adj. = jhallā, q.v. s.m. A large basket.
more: adj. See جهلا 1. Pungent; hot (as cayenne). 2. Ruttish; Iustful; libidinous; warm.

def: s.m. Pumice stone; vitrified brick (com. used to clean the hands and feet with; also written jhāṅwāṅ).
more: s. m. Pumice-stone, bricks burnt to cinder. See جهانوان jhāṅwāṅ.

s.m. = jhāmā, q.v. s.m. Pumice stone; vitrified brick (com. used to clean the hands and feet with; also written jhāṅwāṅ).

def: s.m. Beating rain, a heavy shower; crash (as of breaking glass, &c.); — quickness, celerity, haste, dash, &c. ( = jhapākā, q.v.).
more: n. m. A crash, as of falling glass; a heavy shower.

v.n. To shine, to glitter, glisten, flash; to dance (cf. jhalaknā; ćamaknā).
more: v. n. 1. To glitter. 2. To dance.

s.f. Marshy land, swamp ( = jhāwar, jhābar)

n. m. Illusion; mirage. Dunyā jhāmar jhūmar bhūlī, Satt mān ke bakrā lāë, kān pakaṛ sir kāṭā, Pūjā thī so mālan le gaī, mūrat ko dhar chāṭā . Kabīr. This world's illusion all, its giddy mazes lead astray, In faith a goat is slain upon the shrine its head to lay, The priestess takes it all—left but the stone to lick and pray.
s.m. & adv. = jham-jham, q.v. s.m. & adv. The sound of heavy rain; dashing, beating (of rain), steady downpour; — heavily and continuously (raining).

vocative plural of جَھمیلا (jhamelā)

wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀥𑀚𑁆𑀚𑀡𑁆𑀟 (*dhajjaṇḍa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit *ध्वजदण्ड (*dhvajadaṇḍa, “flagstaff”), from ध्वज (dhvaja) + दण्ड (daṇḍa). Cognate with Bhojpuri झंडा (jhaṇḍā), Punjabi ਝੰਡਾ (jhaṇḍā) / جَھنْڈا (jhanḍā), Sindhi جھَنْڊو (jhanḍo) / झंडो (jhaṇḍo), Gujarati ઝંડો (jhaṇḍo), Marathi झेंडा (jheṇḍā), Nepali झण्डा (jhaṇḍā).

def: n. m. S. जयन्त Dim. jhanḍī . n. f. 1. A flag; banner; an ensign; a standard; flag staff ( nishān ). Telī kā tel, kumhār kā hanḍā; Hāthī kī sūṅḍ, navāb kā jhanḍā . Riddle. Ans. Chirāg . A light. An oilman's oil, and eke the potter's pan, An elephant's trunk, the flag of a nobleman. 2. ( dhan bāl , E.) The male flower of maize ( chaṅvar , W.). jhanḍā gāṛnā , v. a. To fix or plant one's standard; hoist the flag. jhanḍe par chaṛhānā , v. a. To defame; disgrace. jhanḍe par chaṛhnā , v. n. To be exposed ( bāṅs par chaṛhnā , 2). jhanḍe tale kī dostī , n. f. A passing acquaintance. fatah kā jhanḍā , The flag of victory.
s.f. A small flag, a flag; a flagstaff.
s.m. (dialec.), A lock (of hair); petal (of a flower); foliage (of trees).

ety: jhanḍū+lā or ˚lnā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Having a thick or fine head of hair; having thick foliage (a tree); thick, bushy; — a man or child with a thick head of hair; a tree with thick foliage.
more: adj. With thick handsome foliage (a tree), with a fine head of hair (a child).

def: n. 1. A child with a fine head of hair. God jhaṅḍūlā bālak rove, deṭī Rām dukāī; Jo panghaṭ pe chheṛ karegā tujh pe paṛo tavāī! Play. My fine-haired child cries in my arms, hereby on Ram I call, If you insult me at the well may Ruin on you fall! 2. A tree with thick foliage.

ety: jhanḍū+lā or ˚lnā = Prk. उल्लओ = S. उल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. Having a thick or fine head of hair; having thick foliage (a tree); thick, bushy; — a man or child with a thick head of hair; a tree with thick foliage.
Jhang (a city in Punjab, Pakistan)

adj. & s.m. Full of brambles, bushy; — a forest, jungle; the forest of Baijnāth
v.n. To stick or cling (to one) like a bramble; to stick close (to one)

adj. & s.m. Contentious, quarrelsome; — a wrangler, a quarrelsome fellow.

more: n. f. 1. A procession of girls with perforated earthen pots containing a light within in honor of Queen Sānjī. Jhāṅjī le rī jhāṅjī le! sīl sapūtī! jhāṅjī le! Terā beṭā jīve! jhāṅjī le! Girls' song. Jhānjī. Accept our earthen lamp! blessed be with children good, and may your sons all live! our earthen lamp accept! 2. A kind of song so called.
s. f. A kind of play. (Children in the month of Āsin go about at night, dancing, begging, and carrying on their heads earthen pots perforated on all sides, within which lamps are kept burning).

v.n. = jhakhnā = jhaknā, q.v. v.n. To chatter, &c. = jhaknā, q.v. v.n. To babble, chatter, prate, talk nonsense, to rave; to lament; to rail (see jhak mārnā).
v.n. (Braj.) = jhaṅkhnā, jhaknā, q.v. v.n. To be shaken, to be struck or blasted; to be horror-struck, to have the hair standing on end, to be terrified, be scared; to shudder.

more: s. m. A flatterer, wheedler, seducer.

The peacock dancing, capering comes, suffused with shame his legs beholds. (All joy is with some sorrow dashed). 3. To be withered, faded; to faint. Bharat hīṅ dehhi mātu uṭhī dhāī, Murchhit avanī parī jhaṅvāī . Rāmāyan.
v. n. 1. To tan (in the sun). 2. v. a. To wash feet by rubbing جهانوان jhāṅwāṅ, q. v.

1. v. a. To fan, to move to and fro (a fan). 2. v. n. To spring (as, a tiger). 3. To snatch. 4. To wink.
